title: payment — published macros
description: The payment module publishes ZERO framework macros. Every consumer touches the payment surface via injected services (PaymentIntentOrchestrator, PaymentProviderManager) or the domain-event listener path.
rationale: Payment is a TRANSACTIONAL FORWARDER + LEDGER PARTICIPANT. The consumer contract is: emit a domain event (invoice finalized → intent created) or call a service (customer's SDK confirms an intent → capture flows). There is no request-level payment state that needs surfacing as a Request macro (contrast with facility::activeFacility which caches a per-request model).

The current payment intent for a request is NOT request-scoped — it's a per-invoice concept that spans multiple requests (create + confirm + succeed happen across 3+ round-trips). Any request-scoped 'current intent' macro would encourage anti-patterns (SDK state duplicated on the server).

Adding a payment-specific macro (e.g. Request::activePaymentIntent) would signal a design smell — payment intents are stateful conversation objects with the provider, not persistent per-request state.
Provider driver lookup is also NOT macro-worthy — it's DI-injected via PaymentProviderManager. Consumers requiring a driver should inject the manager + call ->forConfig() explicitly.
